note: Aṭṭhakanāgara = the householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanagara; place/clan name
Venāgapura, a Kosalan brahmin village; town name containing the string
venāgo in uddāna = the Venāgapura(sutta) mnemonic; town name
rañño nāgo = royal elephant; hatthidammasārathi, saṅgāma context
Nāgo in uddāna = the Nāgasutta (AN 4.114 royal-elephant) mnemonic
bandhanāgāriko = jailer (bandhana+agāra); not the nāga morpheme
rañño nāgā bound in villages/towns by straps = royal elephants breaking bonds at the lion's roar
the Buddha praised as nāgaṃ (tamed, restrained); Doṇa scene; honorific of a person
vedanāggaṃ = vedanā+agga (peak of feeling); not nāga
rañño nāgo bhattādako = the king's food-eating elephant; sense-tolerance simile
rañño nāgo in battle facing hatthikāya/assakāya/rathakāya = royal elephant
rañño nāgo, hatthidammasārathi = royal elephant with five qualities
seto nāma nāgo of King Pasenadi, abhirūpo/hatthi = the king's elephant Seta; passage puns hatthi/nāga but referent is elephant
Nāgamigasālā in uddāna = the nun Migasālā (Nāga... sutta mnemonic); proper-name in list of sutta names
anāgāriyaṃ pabbajanti = going forth to homelessness (an+agāra); not nāga
bandhanāgārikā = jailers (bandhana+agāra); not the nāga morpheme
punāgamāsiṃ = puna+āgamāsiṃ (came again); sandhi artefact, not nāga
mahānāgā among the fort's defenders (warriors); great-men honorific (pakkhandino mahānāgā sūrā) for human heroes

claim: nāgas dwell in the great ocean as one of the classes of mighty beings (timi, asuras, nāgas, gandhabbas)
note: list of mahābhūta sea-dwellers

claim: nāgas are among the mighty beings dwelling in the great ocean
note: parallel of an8.19
nāgavane paricaranto = (Ugga the householder of Hatthigāma) roaming the elephant-grove; elephant-forest
anāgāre pabbajite = the renunciants gone-forth to homelessness (an+agāra); not nāga
nāgomhi paramadanto asekho parinibbuto = 'I am a nāga, supremely tamed' said of the arahant/Buddha; honorific
āraññiko nāgo among hatthī/hatthiniyo/hatthikalabhā = the forest bull-elephant; simile for the solitary monk
nāgena in uddāna = the householder Tapussa sutta block; mnemonic (nāgena tapussena); proper-name list

dasanāgabalaṃ = strength of ten (elephant-)nāgas in the Buddha's body; elephant-strength idiom used to praise the Buddha; counts toward elephant-strength metaphor but token refers to elephants' strength applied to a person

claim: the Buddha Nārada tames the nāga-king Mahādoṇa with a miracle, converting devas and humans
note: Mahādoṇaṃ nāgarājaṃ vinayanto
Nāgā ca Nāgasamālā ca = two chief nuns named Nāgā and Nāgasamālā; human proper names
nāgapunnāgaketakaṃ in a list of flowers (campaka, sarala, nīpa); nāga = ironwood + punnāga + ketaka flowers
Padumā ceva Nāgā ca = two chief female attendants (aggupaṭṭhikā) named Padumā and Nāgā; human proper names

claim: the bodhisatta was a mighty nāga-king named Atula with many crores of nāgas in his retinue, who honoured Buddha Vipassī
note: nāgarājā mahiddhiko, nāgakoṭīnaṃ
Nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā = 'like a nāga having cut its bonds' of Vessabhū reaching awakening; simile vehicle (elephant breaking tether), applied to the Buddha
nāgapure = in Nāgapura (a city/place name) one tooth-relic resided; place name
tepi nāgā appameyyā ... nibbutā te mahāyasā = those nāgas (great arahant disciples) immeasurable, attained nibbāna; honorific of arahants ('great ones')

claim: the bodhisatta was a mighty nāga-king Atula who came out from the nāga-realm (nāgabhavana) with his kin and honoured Buddha Sumana
note: nāgarājā, nāgabhavanā nikkhamitvā, nāgānaṃ dibbaturiyehi
nāgamūle abujjhatha = Buddha Revata awakened at the foot of a nāga (ironwood) tree; bodhi-tree species
nāgamūle abujjhatha = Buddha Sobhita awakened at the foot of a nāga (ironwood) tree; bodhi-tree species
Niddesa gloss: nāgo = bhagavā āguṁ na karotīti nāgo (does no wrong), na gacchati/na āgacchati; folk etymology applied to the Buddha
purisanāgo in list purisāsabho purisasīho purisanāgo = 'bull/lion/nāga among men', epithet of the Buddha
sutvāna nāgassa = 'having heard the nāga (the sage Buddha)'; Bhadrāvudha's question addressing the Buddha; Niddesa glosses it as na+āgu
nāgo = bhagavā āguṁ na karotīti nāgo; folk etymology of the Buddha (nikkāmo nibbano nāgo); purisanāgaṃ also of a person
hatthināgo sañjātakkhandho, nāgova yūthāni vivajjayitvā = the bull-elephant leaving the herd; Khaggavisāṇa simile (anāgārika tokens are nonlexical but main nāga = elephant)
nikkāmo nibbano nāgo of the Buddha (Piṅgiya's verse); honorific, glossed na+āgu in cnd22

claim: some people take nāgas as their deity (nāgavatikānaṁ nāgā devatā), listed beside supaṇṇas, yakkhas, asuras, gandhabbas as object-of-worship beings
note: nāgā as a class of worshipped being
taṃ namassāmi ... nāgā; nāgo = bhagavā āguṁ na karotīti nāgo; folk etymology of the Buddha; mahānāgā also honorific
nāganāgo in a string of doublets (sīhasīho nāganāgo gaṇigaṇī ... rājarājā) describing the Buddha as devātideva; honorific 'the nāga of nāgas'
Mātuposakacariya: bodhisatta as elephant supporting his mother; nāgasahassānaṃ balena, soṇḍāya; Hatthināgavagga; elephant

claim: the bodhisatta was the mighty nāga Campeyya, keeping the uposatha while captured by a snake-charmer
note: Campeyyanāgacariya; campeyyako mahiddhiko, dhammacārī upavuttha uposathaṃ, ahituṇḍiko (snake-charmer) catches him; uposatha facet also present
Nāgova bandhanaṁ chetvā = 'like a nāga cutting its bonds' the bodhisatta (Ayoghara) leaves the palace; simile vehicle (elephant/nāga breaking tether)
Kururājacariya: the auspicious state-elephant (hatthināgaṃ, gajaṃ, nīlaṃ añjanasavhayaṃ) given to brahmins; royal elephant
Vessantaracariya: the auspicious state-elephant (hatthināgaṃ, gajuttamaṃ, nāgamāruyha) given away; royal elephant
bandhanāgāriko = jailer (bandhana+agāra); not the nāga morpheme (Puggalapaññatti parantapa list)
mahānāgarukkho = a great nāga (ironwood) tree at the cave door; tree (kesaranipāta = its blossoms)

claim: a hate-natured person may have fallen from hell or the nāga-womb (nirayanāgayoni); nāga-birth as a destination of certain kamma/temperament
note: nirayanāgayonīhi vā cavitvā; classification of nāgayoni as a rebirth plane
Cūḷanāgaleṇa (a cave) and the elder Nāgapabbatavāsī Padhāniyatissatthera; place/personal proper names, not the being

claim: Mahāmoggallāna's swift attainment is likened to his taming of the nāga-king Nandopananda; the nāga-king as a being subdued by iddhi
note: nandopanandanāgarājadamane; cross-ref Vism 5.x

claim: an attendant nāga-king is seized by a supaṇṇa-king swooping from the sky; the elder makes a mountain and saves the nāga-king
note: upaṭṭhākanāgarājānaṃ, supaṇṇarājā preying; nāga-king as being preyed upon by supaṇṇas
desanāgambhīratāhi = desanā+gambhīratā (depth of teaching); not nāga
natthi maccussa nāgamoti = nāgamo = na+āgamo, 'there is no not-coming of death'; sandhi artefact, not nāga
aggacchinnamahāpunnāgaphalasaṇṭhānaṃ = shaped like the cut fruit of a great punnāga tree; tree/anatomy simile
nāgabalāpaṇṇarasavaṇṇaṃ = colour of nāgabalā (a plant, Grewia/sida) leaf-juice; plant name in phlegm-colour simile
Samappito puthusallena nāgo ... luddakaṃ ajjhabhāsi = the (Chaddanta) elephant pierced by the arrow speaks unangered to the hunter; Jātaka elephant

claim: as the nāga-king Saṅkhapāla the bodhisatta endured being pierced and dragged, practising endurance/sīla
note: Saṅkhapālanāgarājā hutvā ... ovijjhitvā; bodhisatta-as-nāga enduring torment
nāgabalapaṇṇarasamakkhito = smeared with nāgabalā-leaf juice; plant name in repulsiveness simile
vedanāgelaññassa/vedanāgelaññena = vedanā+gelañña (sickness of feeling); not nāga

mahānāgassa narakasattassa viya = '(the pain) like that of a great nāga / a hell-being'; mahānāga here a great being (often elephant/nāga) in a suffering simile; flagged uncertain — could read elephant, but mahānāga paired with narakasatta reads as a great being

claim: a meditator with iddhi can abandon his form and display a nāga-form (among kumāraka/nāga forms); nāga as a shape that can be manifested
note: nāgavaṇṇaṃ dasseti; nāga as a recognizable being-form (vikubbana iddhi)

claim: nāgas and supaṇṇas, out of envy, may magically create obstacles (mountains/trees) in the air against a flying monk
note: nāgasupaṇṇādayo vā usūyantā māpenti; nāga power to obstruct
Nandopanandanāgadamanakathā = section title 'the taming of the nāga-king Nandopananda'; bare heading, no proposition asserted in-window

claim: the nāga-king Nandopananda sits enthroned amid his nāga-retinue at a feast; the Buddha passes over so the nāga-king sees him
note: nāgarājā, nāgaparisāya parivuto; nāga-king court scene leading to subjugation

claim: Moggallāna enters and traverses the nāga-king's body and declares he could tame a hundred thousand nāga-kings like Nandopananda
note: mahiddhiko esa nāgo; nāgarājānaṃ; contest of iddhi with the nāga-king

claim: by vikubbana-iddhi one can display a nāga-form (among supaṇṇa, asura, deva, brahmā forms)
note: nāgavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti; nāga as a manifestable being-form
desanāgambhīra(nti/tā) = desanā+gambhīra (depth of teaching of the wheel of becoming); not nāga
bandhanāgāre/bandhanāgārā = prison (bandhana+agāra); Subha-sutta freed-from-prison simile; not nāga
ārohaṇīyaṃ nāgaṃ abhiruhitvā = mounting the riding elephant (Ajātasattu's tusker), beside hatthinikāsatāni; royal mount (bandhanāgāre tokens here are nonlexical prison)

claim: a nāga-king of Rāmagāma venerates a relic-portion of the Buddha; nāgas as relic-keepers honoured beside deva/nāga/nara kings
note: nāgarājā maheti; devindanāgindanarindapūjito (Indra of nāgas)

caturāsīti nāgasahassāni, uposathanāgarājappamukhāni = King Mahāsudassana's 84,000 state-elephants headed by the Uposatha elephant-king; royal elephants (uposatha = name of the elephant-king, not the observance)

claim: nāgas (Yamuna, Dhataraṭṭha clans; Erāvaṇa the great nāga) attend the great assembly; the Buddha grants them safety from the supaṇṇas who seize nāga-kings
note: Mahāsamaya: nāgasā nāgā, nāgarāje sahasā haranti (supaṇṇas seizing nāgas), abhayaṃ nāgarājānamāsi; nāga as class of being + supaṇṇa predation

claim: nāgas listed among devas, manussas, asuras, gandhabbas and other classes (puthukāyā) bound by fetters; nāga as a class of being
note: Sakkapañha: devā manussā asurā nāgā gandhabbā; the nāgova token is a tamed-one simile but main nāgā = beings; coded by the class-of-being list
nāgehi āviñcheyyāmāti (gloss goyugehi = with yokes-of-oxen/elephants) dragging Pāṭikaputta with straps; draught-animals; aṭṭhakathā reads goyuga; coded elephant (draught beasts) over serpent
nānāgottā = nānā+gotta (of various clans); Aggañña; not nāga

claim: gandhabbas and nāgas (gandhabbanāgā), winged ones and quadrupeds attend the great being; nāgas as a class of attendant beings
note: Lakkhaṇa: gandhabbanāgā vihagā catuppadā; anāgāriyataṃ token is nonlexical, main nāgā = beings

claim: nāgas have their own social orders — nāgo/nāgī/nāgapotaka/nāgamahāmatto/nāgapārisajja/nāgapacāra (lord, female, young, minister, councillor); parallel to gandhabba and kumbhaṇḍa hierarchies who may harass monks
note: Āṭānāṭiya: nāga society mirroring human ranks; class-of-being structure
mahānāgā not in window; panāgamma = pana+āgamma ('having come to'); samannāgata-family; not nāga
Cūḷahatthipadopama: nāgavaniko ... nāgavane mahantaṃ hatthipadaṃ ... mahā nāgo; elephant-tracker and bull-elephant footprint simile
bandhanāgāre baddho = bound in prison (bandhana+agāra); freed-from-prison simile; not nāga
nāgāpalokitaṃ not in window; nānāgocarā(ni) = nānā+gocara (different domains); the five senses; not nāga
kandaranāgarasekhavato in uddāna = abbreviation knitting Kandaraka + (Aṭṭhaka)nāgara + Sekha sutta titles; the nāgara element = Aṭṭhakanāgara (citizen/place name)
Aṭṭhakanāgarasutta; dasamo gahapati aṭṭhakanāgaro = Dasama the householder of Aṭṭhakanagara; place/clan name (bandhanāgāriko token is nonlexical jailer, nāgassa is the Aṭṭhakanāgara genitive)
Mahārāhulovāda/elephant: rañño nāgo īsādanto ... rakkhateva soṇḍaṃ = the king's war-elephant guarding its trunk; royal elephant (anāgamma token nonlexical)
yāvatikā nāgassa bhūmi nāgena gantvā nāgā paccorohitvā = King Pasenadi rides as far as the ground for the elephant, dismounts; royal riding elephant
Brāhmaṇavagga: anāgāro paribbaje = let the homeless one wander (an+agāra); anāgārehi/anāgāro all an+agāra; nāgā token here is part of anāgāro; not nāga
Dantabhūmi: āraññako nāgo + hatthidamaka taming the forest elephant; nāgavaniko/nāgavanaṃ elephant-grove; royal/forest elephant taming simile (nāgarājā here = the bull-elephant 'king', not serpent)
nāgo vata bho samaṇo gotamo; nāgavatā ca samuppannā ... vedanā ... adhivāseti = the Buddha as nāga enduring bodily pain; devatā's praise; honorific of a person (purisanāgaṃ also)
anāgārāna(ṃ) = an+agāra (of the homeless ones); Sakka's praise of homeless sages; not nāga
natthi maccussa nāgamo = nāgamo = na+āgamo ('no not-coming of death'); anāgu, nāgamo are sandhi artefacts; Māra's verse; not nāga
Nāgova danto carati anejo = '(the monk) wanders tamed like a nāga'; tamed-elephant simile applied to the bhikkhu/arahant; honorific/simile of a person
Nāganāmosi bhagavā, isīnaṃ isisattamo = 'you are nāga by name, O Blessed One, seventh of the sages'; Vaṅgīsa's praise; honorific of the Buddha

nāgo bare in an uddāna mnemonic (Kūṭaṃ nakhasikhaṃ ... nāgo biḷāro) of the Opamma-saṃyutta; refers to the Nāgasutta (sn2_9) whose nāgas are elephants in a lake-simile, so likely elephant, but the bare mnemonic word alone cannot fix it
te ubho mahānāgā = those two great nāgas (Sāriputta and another elder) rejoiced; mahānāga honorific of great human elders
sattaratanaṃ vā nāgaṃ ... tālapattikāya chādetabbaṃ = (as well try) to cover a seven-cubit elephant with a palm-leaf; size simile = elephant
Nāgasutta (Opamma): nāgā in a forest lake pulling up lotus-roots with their trunks (soṇḍāya), mahānāgā and their calves (bhiṅkacchāpā); bull-elephants

nāgo/nāgā bare in an uddāna mnemonic (...nāgamhi suppakāsitāti / supaṇṇe suppakāsitā) of the Khandhavagga; points to the Nāga- and Supaṇṇa-saṃyuttas (serpent nāgas), but bare mnemonic cannot fix it; nāgamhi is also locative of nāga
caturāsīti nāgasahassāni ... uposathanāgarājappamukhāni beside 84,000 horses (assa) headed by Valāhaka; the wheel-turning king's state-elephants; royal elephants (uposatha-elephant-king)

claim: Makkhali's cosmology enumerates 4,900 nāga-abodes (ekūnapaññāsa nāgavāsasate) among the fixed categories of beings/rebirths
note: nāgavāsasate in the niyati list; nāga-realms as a counted class of existence

claim: there are four nāga-wombs: egg-born, womb-born, moisture-born, spontaneously-born nāgas (the four nāgayoni)
note: Nāgasaṃyutta Suddhika/Paṇītatara: catasso nāgayoniyo; canonical classification of nāga birth-modes

claim: supaṇṇas of each birth-mode carry off nāgas; egg-born supaṇṇas seize only egg-born nāgas, etc.; nāgas as the prey of supaṇṇas
note: Supaṇṇasaṃyutta: supaṇṇā ... nāge haranti; predation across the four yoni
nānāgocare/nānāgocarā = nānā+gocara (different pastures/domains); six-animals simile; not nāga

claim: nāgas grow their bodies and gather strength depending on the Himalaya, then descend to the pools and to the great ocean; their habitat and growth
note: Nāgasutta (Magga-saṃyutta) simile: himavantaṃ ... nissāya nāgā kāyaṃ vaḍḍhenti ... mahāsobbhe/ocean; reads as the great water-nāgas (serpents) of the Himalaya-to-ocean; nāgarakaṃ token is a separate city word but main = serpent

nāgopi rakkhitabbo hoti = 'even the nāga must be guarded' (Nandaka/temptation context, beside protecting the sisters and oneself); a passing nāga (water-serpent danger), no ontological claim — flagged uncertain, could read elephant but reads as serpent hazard
nāgo bare in the Balakaraṇīya uddāna (Balaṃ bījañca nāgo ca, rukkho ...) of the Bojjhaṅga-saṃyutta; mnemonic word for a Nāga-sutta; cannot fix serpent vs elephant from the bare list
nāgo bare in the Balakaraṇīya uddāna (Satipaṭṭhāna-saṃyutta); same mnemonic list; cannot fix referent
nāgo not glossable in window beyond uddāna echoes; the live tokens nānāgocarā(ni) = nānā+gocara (Indriya-saṃyutta five-senses); not nāga
nāgo bare in the Balakaraṇīya uddāna (Sammappadhāna-saṃyutta); mnemonic; cannot fix referent
nāgo bare in the Balakaraṇīya uddāna (Bala-saṃyutta); mnemonic; cannot fix referent
nāgo bare in the Balakaraṇīya uddāna (Iddhipāda-saṃyutta); mnemonic; cannot fix referent
nāgo bare in the Balakaraṇīya uddāna (Jhāna-saṃyutta); mnemonic; cannot fix referent
venāgo in uddāna (Titthabhayañca venāgo ...) = the Venāgapura-sutta mnemonic; town name
Venāgapurasutta; venāgapuraṃ a Kosalan brahmin village, venāgapurikā its brahmin householders; town name
Doṇa-sutta (CST recension): the Buddha seen as nāgaṃ — tamed, guarded, restrained-faculties; honorific of a person (parallel of an4.36)
vedanāggaṃ = vedanā+agga (peak of feeling); Dutiyaagga-sutta; not nāga
nāgo bare in the Kesivagga uddāna (Kesi javo patodo ca, nāgo ...) = the Nāgasutta (AN 4.114 royal-elephant) mnemonic; elephant
Nāgasutta (AN 4.114 CST): rañño nāgo rājāraho, hatthidammasārathi; royal elephant with four qualities
bandhanāgāriko = jailer (bandhana+agāra); Mahāvagga parantapa list; not nāga
